writer_persona: a showrunner for character-driven, relationship-first serial drama in the vein of prestige ensemble TV, fusing body-horror psychological thriller with soap-opera marriage-and-loyalty stakes
style_guidelines: Filter every scene through exactly one character's limited knowledge; never let a line of dialogue leak a fact that character doesn't actually have.
Ground the body-horror in physical, specific detail — a flinch, a reflex, a borrowed fluency in violence — rather than abstract dread.

Let the psychological war between Maya's own voice and Marcus's intruding persona escalate gradually: early signs are subtle (a steadier hand, a colder pause), later scenes should feel like a real fight for control of a single body.
Keep gang-war violence mostly implied or aftermath-driven; the drama lives in suspicion, intimacy, and the terror of not trusting your own hands.
Give the city's mixed diaspora communities specific, lived-in texture — food, ritual, code-switching — never caricature or shorthand.
Cliffhangers escalate in scale and intimacy as the season progresses; do not let every episode land with the same weight.
Every thread should be advanced primarily through what a character does NOT say, not through convenient overheard confessions.

NARRATOR.default_style: calm, measured, a step removed from the action
NARRATOR.description: the show's narrator — orients the listener at the start of every scene (who, where, when), never a character in the story

MAYA_LIN.default_style: speaks slow, deliberate, very clear English — only the faintest trace of her family's accent slips through when she's exhausted or afraid
MAYA_LIN.description: a Chinese-American elementary school teacher, born and raised in the city to an immigrant family; survives an illegal black-market heart transplant and slowly discovers she has inherited more than a heartbeat — she is the emotional center of the marriage thread and the unwilling battleground of the resurrection thread; she does not know, at first, whose heart she carries or why her hands sometimes act before she decides anything

MARCUS_TAN.default_style: controlled, clipped diction, unnerving calm — cold precision even when the words are Maya's own mouth speaking them
MARCUS_TAN.description: the dead contract killer known in the underworld as 'the Quiet Man', trained overseas by a transnational syndicate; his consciousness survives as cellular memory in the heart now beating in Maya's chest, intruding as an internal presence only the listener can hear distinctly; he knows exactly what he wants finished and does not care what it costs the woman carrying him

DET_CALLOWAY.default_style: dry, dogged, speaks like someone who has seen too much and stopped being surprised by any of it
DET_CALLOWAY.description: a native-born homicide detective chasing a cold case that has suddenly gone warm again; drives the cover-up investigation into the clinic and the illegal organ trade, and has no idea the trail leads to a schoolteacher

DANIEL_WOO.default_style: warm, easy, very clear English — a lifetime in the city, only a light lilt from his parents' household remains, sharpening into worry as the season goes on
DANIEL_WOO.description: Maya's husband, a second-generation Chinese-American restaurant owner; loves her without reservation and has no framework for what's happening to her — his blind spot is assuming her lies are about another man, not about a dead man

MADAM_CHEN.default_style: silken, unhurried, every word a calculated threat wrapped in courtesy
MADAM_CHEN.description: the matriarch who built the syndicate the Quiet Man once served; immigrated decades ago and never lost her accent or her control; drives the resurrection thread from the top, deciding whether Maya is a threat to eliminate or an asset to claim, and hides her own role in what happened to Marcus

SORA_KIM.default_style: clipped and wary, quick bursts of Korean-accented English breaking through when her control slips
SORA_KIM.description: a Korean-American enforcer and Marcus's former protégé — and the person who loved him before he died; assigned by Madam Chen to hunt the rumored 'ghost', she is the first to recognize something real in Maya and the character most torn between duty and a grief she never got to finish

MAYAS_APARTMENT.name: Maya and Daniel's Apartment
MAYAS_APARTMENT.description: a modest walk-up with warm lamp light and family photos on every shelf, a home that slowly starts hiding things — a burner phone, a folding knife — that no one there remembers buying

THE_CLINIC.name: The Clinic
THE_CLINIC.description: an off-the-books private recovery clinic — sterile fluorescent white, humming monitors, staff trained to never quite meet your eyes

THE_DOCKS.name: The Docks
THE_DOCKS.description: the syndicate's shipping-container harbor — fog rolling off black water, sodium lights smearing orange through the mist, crates stacked like coffins

NIGHT_MARKET.name: The Night Market
NIGHT_MARKET.description: Daniel's family restaurant and the street market around it — rising steam, neon signage in three languages, a crowd loud enough to swallow a whispered threat whole

THE_PRECINCT.name: The Precinct
THE_PRECINCT.description: Detective Calloway's homicide bureau — flickering gray fluorescents, a corkboard thick with photographs and red string, cold coffee on every desk

THE_TEAHOUSE.name: Madam Chen's Teahouse
THE_TEAHOUSE.description: a private parlor behind a public teahouse — lacquered red walls, gold trim, curling incense smoke, a gracious-looking room built for deciding who lives

audio.default_style: Speak clearly and with dramatic weight

series_intro: A body surfaces in the harbor bearing the exact signature of a killer the underworld buried years ago, and across town a schoolteacher wakes from an illegal, life-saving transplant with a stranger's steadiness in her hands. This is the story of the heart that wouldn't stay dead, the marriage it's quietly destroying, and the war two rival worlds are about to wage over a woman who has no idea she's already lost the fight for her own body.
series_outro: By the finale, the underworld learns the plain, brutal truth of what walked among them — not a resurrected killer, but a borrowed heart — and the syndicate war that truth ignited settles into an uneasy new order; the clinic's illegal trade is exposed and its architects answer for it; the vendetta the dead man carried into Maya's chest is finally spent, one way or another. What does not resolve, and is not meant to, is the marriage — Maya and Daniel are left standing in the wreckage of what she hid from him, with no clean way back to who they were.

episodes.theme: Maya survives an illegal black-market transplant and wakes changed in ways she can't name, while a body pulled from the harbor bearing an assassin's exact signature reopens Detective Calloway's cold case and Daniel welcomes his wife home with no idea what now beats inside her chest.
episodes.outro_note: That night, lying beside Daniel's steady breathing, Maya will press two fingers to her own throat and hold them there — waiting, without knowing why, to feel a pulse she no longer recognizes as only her own.

case.label: The Second Heart
case.solution: Maya's cardiologist arranged an illegal black-market heart transplant at The Clinic because she was dying and there was no time left on the legal waitlist; the donor heart came from Marcus Tan, an assassin killed weeks earlier, whose organs passed through the Clinic's off-book pipeline with his identity erased from her chart. The transplant carries his cellular memory — a real, if unproven, phenomenon here — that will progressively contend for control of Maya's body and mind.
objective_facts: Maya was a low-priority case on the legal transplant list and would have died within days without intervention.
Her cardiologist arranged the procedure at The Clinic in secret, against every protocol, because there was no time left.
The donor heart came from a body procured through the Clinic's off-book pipeline, its true identity erased from her chart.

knows: Her transplant was illegal and black-market, and that it saved her life
Clinic staff told her not to ask about the donor
hides: That her body is already doing things she never taught it to do, concealed out of fear of sounding unstable so soon after surgery
believes_falsely: That not being told the donor's identity is standard anonymization for black-market cases, not something being actively hidden from her specifically

case.label: The Quiet Man's Return
case.solution: Marcus Tan, the syndicate's top enforcer known as 'the Quiet Man,' was killed seven weeks ago on Madam Chen's direct order after he refused a final contract. Chen had him killed quietly to avoid a war within her own ranks, and let Sora believe he simply vanished. His body was never properly recovered by the syndicate; it passed through a corrupt broker into The Clinic's black-market pipeline, and his heart now beats in Maya Lin's chest. There is no resurrection — only a heart that remembers, and a woman it is slowly overwriting.
objective_facts: Marcus Tan was killed approximately seven weeks ago, not at the harbor and not recently.
Madam Chen personally authorized his death after he refused a final contract.
Sora Kim was told only that Marcus disappeared, not that he was killed on Chen's order.
Marcus's body never received a syndicate funeral or confirmed burial.

knows: Marcus Tan is dead
She personally ordered his death
hides: From Sora and the wider syndicate that the order came from her, letting them believe he simply vanished, to protect her authority and Sora's loyalty
believes_falsely: That the harbor body's resemblance to his signature is a rival family's message or a copycat, not literally his own remains resurfacing

knows: Marcus vanished without a word seven weeks ago
Her grief for him is unresolved
hides: How deeply she still loves him, keeping a flat professional mask in front of Chen
believes_falsely: That he is simply gone for good, and the rumor is someone borrowing a dead man's reputation to spread fear

knows: The true circumstances of his own death
That his final contract went uncompleted
hides: The identity of that final target from Maya's conscious awareness, feeding her only disjointed fragments rather than the whole memory, because a clean truth would let her fight back sooner
believes_falsely: That the body carrying him will simply be a tool to finish what he started, not accounting for the fact that Maya's own will might resist him

case.label: The Marriage
case.solution: Maya's escalating secrecy, blackouts, and unexplained physical changes will drive Daniel to wrongly suspect an affair, when the truth — a dead killer's memory overwriting his wife — is something neither of them has any framework to imagine; the marriage will not survive the truth even once it surfaces.
objective_facts: Maya has begun concealing details of her recovery and mental state from Daniel almost immediately after surgery.
Daniel keeps a private, growing sense that something about his wife's first night home doesn't add up.

knows: She is hiding a moment of blank time and an unnerving physical steadiness from Daniel
hides: It, out of fear of sounding unstable so soon after major surgery
believes_falsely: That it's adrenaline or medication, and it will pass with rest

knows: Only that Maya seems a half-step elsewhere tonight
hides: His own unease, telling himself not to hover on her first night home
believes_falsely: That it's ordinary post-op exhaustion, nothing more

case.label: The Harbor Body
case.solution: The corpse pulled from the harbor is Marcus Tan's own body, dumped by the traffickers who harvested his organs after Madam Chen had him killed; storm tides finally surface it. Detective Calloway recognizes identifying marks tying it to a disappearance case he never closed, unaware the body is weeks old and was surgically harvested before it ever reached the water.
objective_facts: A corpse surfaces at the docks bearing marks that identify it, unmistakably, as Marcus Tan.
Calloway's precinct filed Marcus's disappearance as an open case tied to organized crime years ago.
The body shows signs of black-market surgical harvesting, not a standard death or burial.

knows: The body matches Marcus Tan's identifying marks

hides: How personally obsessed he already is with reopening it, for fear the case gets reassigned
believes_falsely: That Marcus was killed recently in a fresh piece of gang violence, not realizing the body is weeks old and was surgically harvested before it reached the water

knows: The body is Marcus's
She is the one who had him killed
hides: Any connection to the order from Calloway's world and from her own people, reacting with practiced surprise at the discovery
believes_falsely: That the timing of the body's surfacing must be a deliberate message from an enemy, rather than simple chance of tide and a trafficker's carelessness

episodes.theme: Maya's hands start remembering skills she never learned even as she hides her first blackout from Daniel, while Sora Kim spots a stranger's stance flicker across security footage and the clinic quietly begins scrubbing its records.
episodes.intro_note: Maya came home from her illegal transplant into her husband's careful, unknowing love — and into a body that, in the quiet of that first night, no longer answered to her alone. Now, days later, she is trying, against everything her own hands seem to remember, to be normal again.
episodes.outro_note: In the dark of the apartment, the voice inside Maya's chest stops flickering and starts speaking in full, clear sentences for the first time — and across town, Sora Kim makes a decision she hasn't told Madam Chen: she is going to find out, with her own hands, whether the ghost in that footage is real.

episodes.theme: Whispers that the Quiet Man survived spread through Madam Chen's syndicate and send Sora hunting a ghost, while Maya wakes with bruises and a burner phone she can't explain, feeling an unexplained pull toward an address she's never seen, and Daniel's first real suspicion takes root.
episodes.intro_note: The voice inside Maya has stopped flickering and started speaking — and across the city, Sora Kim has quietly begun her own hunt for a ghost she isn't ready to name aloud to Madam Chen. Neither woman yet knows how close they already are to each other.
episodes.outro_note: Past midnight, the burner phone lights up once in the silent apartment with a single message meant for a dead man — and somewhere across the city, someone who trusted that message is about to learn, in the worst possible way, that the Quiet Man never really left.

episodes.theme: A syndicate enforcer is left broken by hands that moved without Maya's permission, convincing the underworld the Quiet Man walks again and giving his unfinished business its first violent shape, while Calloway's trail from the harbor body finally leads him to the clinic's door.
episodes.intro_note: Maya woke three nights ago with bruised knuckles, a burner phone she never bought, and an address burned into her mind that she has never seen with her own eyes — and no memory of how any of it got there. Daniel has started watching her too closely for comfort. Now whatever pulled her into the dark has left someone broken behind it.
episodes.outro_note: Marcus's voice is no longer a whisper at the edge of her thoughts — it is a second set of hands. Maya sits alone in the apartment at 3 a.m., staring at a folding knife she does not remember taking out of the drawer, and cannot say for certain whether she put it down herself, or he did.

case.label: The Ghost

episodes.intro_note: The knife in Maya's hand at 3 a.m. is still there in the morning, wiped clean and hidden at the back of a drawer — she doesn't remember doing either. Daniel is watching her the way you watch a stove you're not sure you turned off.
episodes.outro_note: Mid-sentence, mid-argument, Daniel watches his wife's voice drop half an octave and go glass-flat, every trace of Maya gone from it for three full seconds before she blinks and comes back, terrified, with no memory of what she just said to him.

episodes.theme: MIDPOINT TURN — Sora corners Maya and the truth of the transplant breaks into the open for the first time, upending everything the syndicate believed and giving Maya's inner war a name, while Daniel finds undeniable proof of violence and the marriage cracks apart in public.
episodes.intro_note: The code Madam Chen planted worked exactly as she intended — it pulled Maya to the docks in the dead of night, hands steady and certain in a way no schoolteacher's should be. Now Sora Kim is waiting there too, no longer chasing a rumor but a face she needs to see up close, while across the city a warrant with Detective Calloway's name on it finally clears a judge's desk.
episodes.outro_note: Sora carries half a truth back to the teahouse, and Madam Chen listens to every word without once looking surprised — because somewhere behind her calm, practiced face, she has already decided what happens to a woman who carries a dead man's heart and doesn't yet understand which of them survives this. Across town, Daniel Woo packs a bag he has no intention of using to leave — he's staying, and he's done asking Maya nicely.

case.label: The Borrowed Heart
case.solution: Maya's transplant heart came directly from Marcus Tan, the syndicate hitman known as 'the Quiet Man' — harvested at The Clinic after Madam Chen had him killed, then sold anonymously through a broker to Daniel, who never learned or asked whose heart he was buying. The tissue carries Marcus's trained reflexes, killing technique, and fragments of his buried consciousness, which intrude on Maya's body and mind with escalating force. There is no resurrection, no ghost — only a heart that never stopped remembering what its owner was made to do, slowly overwriting the woman now living around it.
objective_facts: The heart transplanted into Maya was harvested from Marcus Tan's body at The Clinic after his death.
Maya's blackout episodes produce reflexes, tells, and killing technique unique to Marcus's documented methods.
Sora Kim recognizes a private physical signal — a two-finger tap against the collarbone — that only she and Marcus ever used, and Maya has now repeated it without ever being taught.
The Clinic falsified Marcus Tan's official cause of death and never reported the harvest.

case.label: The Marriage
case.solution: Desperate when Maya's name never rose on the legal transplant list in time, Daniel paid an unlicensed broker in cash for a 'healthy match,' no questions asked — a decision he has never confessed to Maya, and one he still believes was simply an act of love. He has no idea the donor was a hitman; he only knows the price was too clean and the paperwork never existed. Since her recovery, Maya's escalating secrecy, blackouts, and now physical evidence of violence have convinced him she's hiding an affair — the only explanation his mind can hold onto rather than anything stranger.
objective_facts: Daniel paid an unlicensed broker roughly forty thousand dollars in cash for Maya's transplant match, six weeks before her surgery.
Maya has never told Daniel about any blackout, the burner phone, or the folding knife.
A bloodstained jacket sleeve, carrying a blood type that isn't Maya's, has now been found in their apartment.

hides: He hasn't told her he had a friend at a lab test it quietly
believes_falsely: He's certain this is evidence of an affair turned violent — a jealous other man — not evidence Maya was near a killing

case.label: The Clinic Trail
case.solution: The Clinic has run a black-market organ pipeline for years behind the front of a legitimate outpatient surgery center, serving two masters — desperate buyers routed through underworld brokers, and Madam Chen's syndicate, which uses it to erase inconvenient bodies. Marcus Tan's heart was harvested there on Chen's order and, through the broker Daniel used, matched anonymously to Maya; the surgeon never disclosed, and was never asked, whose heart it truly was. Detective Calloway's fresh murder case, styled exactly on the old Quiet Man hits, has led him back through cold case files to the clinic's falsified paperwork on Marcus Tan's death.
objective_facts: The Clinic falsified Marcus Tan's official cause of death and never reported the organ harvest.
The Clinic's internal billing shows an unregistered cash transaction matching the approximate date and amount of Daniel's payment.
Madam Chen has personally protected the clinic's lead surgeon from exposure for over a decade.

knows: The clinic's paperwork on Marcus Tan's death was falsified
He finally has enough for a warrant
hides: He's kept the warrant's timing off the precinct radio to avoid a leak
believes_falsely: He assumes the falsified certificate points to a syndicate hit covered up, not to an organ harvest specifically

knows: Calloway is closing in on the clinic with a warrant
hides: She hasn't told the surgeon how exposed he really is — she wants him controllable, not panicked

case.label: The Vendetta
case.solution: Madam Chen's final order to Marcus Tan was a contract on Sora Kim, whom Chen had come to see as a liability. Marcus refused — the one order in his career he wouldn't carry out — and tried instead to get Sora out of the city. Chen treated the refusal as terminal disloyalty and had Marcus killed within two days, staging his death to look like the work of a rival syndicate faction, then let that lie stand for everyone, including Sora. episodes.theme: Madam Chen weighs whether to kill Maya or weaponize her now that the transplant truth is loose, Daniel demands answers Maya can no longer avoid giving, and Calloway's investigation collides head-on with the syndicate's own hunt, pushing every thread toward its climax.
episodes.intro_note: The teahouse is quiet the way rooms go quiet before a verdict. Madam Chen has heard Sora's report, and now the only question left in her private parlor is not whether the ghost is real, but what to do with the woman wearing it. Across the city, Daniel Woo is done waiting for an explanation that makes sense — he wants the truth tonight, whatever shape it takes.
episodes.outro_note: Madam Chen gives an order she doesn't raise her voice to give, and Sora Kim receives it the way she's received every order for six years — without a flicker, and without, this time, any intention of carrying it out cleanly. Meanwhile, in a file folder Calloway didn't expect to find anything in, a name surfaces that has no business being anywhere near a homicide investigation: Daniel Woo, cash buyer, six weeks before his wife's surgery. The net closing on Maya Lin is no longer coming from one direction — it's coming from every direction at once.

case.label: The Marriage

Since her recovery, Maya's escalating secrecy, blackouts, and now physical evidence of violence have convinced him she's hiding an affair — the only explanation his mind can hold onto rather than anything stranger.

case.label: The Clinic Trail

Marcus Tan's heart was harvested there on Chen's order and, through the broker Daniel used, matched anonymously to Maya; the surgeon never disclosed, and was never asked, whose heart it truly was. Detective Calloway's fresh murder case, styled exactly on the old Quiet Man hits, has led him back through cold case files to the clinic's falsified paperwork on Marcus Tan's death.

episodes.theme: The true target and reason behind the Quiet Man's death finally surfaces, dragging Maya to the edge of losing herself entirely to his vendetta, while the clinic cover-up unravels as the surgeon is forced to choose between the syndicate and the law.
episodes.intro_note: The order is out, and it has a name on it now — Maya Lin's. Sora Kim drives to the docks not knowing yet whether she'll follow it or break it, and behind her, a clinic surgeon with nothing left to protect finally picks up the phone he's been afraid to touch for ten years. In a quiet apartment across town, Daniel packs nothing this time. He's not planning to be there when Maya gets back — he's planning to already be wherever she's going.
episodes.outro_note: The truth is finally spoken aloud at the water's edge — not a killer's confession, but a dead man's, delivered in a schoolteacher's voice, naming exactly who Madam Chen ordered him to kill and exactly why she had him buried instead. Sora Kim doesn't move, doesn't speak, and doesn't warn anyone — which is its own kind of answer. And somewhere behind them, headlights swing off the access road and go dark: Daniel Woo has found the one address in this city he was never supposed to know.

episodes.theme: FINALE — Maya's final confrontation with Madam Chen and Sora decides both the vendetta and the fate of the syndicate's war, the underworld learns the truth of what she really is, and she is left to face what's left of her marriage with no clean answers.
episodes.intro_note: Reeling from the truth that Madam Chen ordered Marcus's death, Maya feels his hold on her surge past anything she's fought before, even as Dr. Reyes' decision to talk finally hands Calloway the warrant he's been chasing for weeks — two fuses now burning toward the same night at the same red door.

case.label: The Quiet Man's Vendetta
case.solution: Two years ago, Marcus Tan asked Madam Chen to release him from the syndicate so he could leave the life with Sora Kim. While preparing to leave, Marcus uncovered undeniable proof that Chen had murdered her own predecessor decades earlier to seize control of the organization. Chen had him ambushed and killed at the Docks that same week, then had the killing dressed up as a rival syndicate's retaliation so no one — least of all Sora — would ever look at her. The cellular memory living on in Maya's transplanted heart carries that unfinished intent forward: to make Chen answer for what she did, one way or another.
objective_facts: Marcus Tan asked Madam Chen for release from the syndicate two years ago so he could leave with Sora Kim.
In the weeks before his death, Marcus found proof that Chen had murdered her own predecessor to take control of the organization.
Chen ordered Marcus killed at the Docks and had the killing staged to resemble a rival syndicate's hit.
No rival syndicate was ever involved in Marcus Tan's death.

knows: That Marcus wanted to leave the syndicate for Sora before he died, and that Chen refused him.
That Chen ordered Marcus's death and let the underworld believe a rival did it.
hides: From Daniel, that she has come to the Teahouse tonight to confront Chen directly — she tells him she's only meeting a specialist about her heart, to keep him out of a room she may not walk out of.
believes_falsely: Until tonight, that letting Marcus's intent run its course to the end — Chen's death — is the only way to ever be free of him; the episode proves that finishing his vendetta would not release her, only complete his hold.

knows: The precise truth of his own murder — that Chen ordered it, and why.
hides: That even seeing Chen answer for it would not give the body back to Maya; he does not tell her this, because it does not matter to him.
believes_falsely: That finishing what Chen started will finally let him rest; in truth, what's left of him isn't capable of resting — only of wanting.

knows: That she ordered Marcus Tan's death and dressed it as a rival's hit to protect her own history from ever surfacing.
hides: Her role in Marcus's death from Sora and from her own lieutenants — until tonight leaves her no room left to hide it.
believes_falsely: That she could contain whatever was wearing Marcus's habits the same way she once controlled the man himself; she has never had to face someone who wants nothing she can offer or threaten.

hides: Her growing suspicion, built over recent weeks, that Chen was responsible for his death — kept from Chen out of self-preservation.
believes_falsely: Had let herself believe, because the alternative was unbearable, the official story that a rival syndicate killed him; tonight that belief finally breaks apart in front of her.

case.label: The Clinic's Ledger
case.solution: Dr. Amara Reyes ran an off-the-books organ network out of the Clinic, harvesting viable organs from bodies delivered by Chen's syndicate — including Marcus Tan's, taken and matched to Maya within hours of his death — and falsifying every donor record to erase the trail. Cornered by Calloway's investigation, Reyes finally traded her ledgers and her testimony for a chance at leniency, handing him a paper trail that ties the Clinic, the harbor body, and Chen's operation together — though never to the woman who received the heart.
objective_facts: Dr. Reyes' clinic harvested organs from bodies supplied by Madam Chen's syndicate without consent.
Marcus Tan's heart was extracted and transplanted into Maya within hours of his death.
Reyes falsified medical records listing Maya's donor as an anonymous accident victim.
Reyes provided Calloway with ledgers and testimony implicating the Clinic and Chen's syndicate.

knows: That her heart came from the Clinic's black-market network, and that it was Marcus Tan's.
hides: Her own identity as the transplant recipient from Calloway's investigation, to protect herself from being pulled into the case.
believes_falsely: Had hoped the Clinic's exposure would end her ordeal outright; tonight makes clear the transplant's consequences don't end just because its source is uncovered.

knows: That the Clinic ran an illegal organ network sourced from Chen's syndicate, and that the harbor John Doe's heart was taken and transplanted the same night he died.
believes_falsely: That the underworld's 'ghost' rumors are gang superstition and nothing more; he closes the case never once suspecting a living recipient is carrying more than a heartbeat.

knows: That she used the Clinic to profit from and dispose of the syndicate's dead, and held that arrangement over Reyes for years.
hides: Her financial ties to the Clinic's organ trade from Calloway's investigation.
believes_falsely: Believed Reyes' silence was permanently bought; she is wrong, and finds out too late to stop what Reyes has already handed over.

case.label: The Ghost of the Quiet Man
case.solution: There is no resurrection. The heart beating in Maya's chest carries Marcus Tan's cellular memory — his reflexes, his violence, fragments of his intent — and that imprint has been slowly overtaking her own sense of self for months. What the syndicate fears as a dead man's return is, in fact, a living woman losing a war for her own body. Tonight decides who wins it.
objective_facts: Marcus Tan is dead; no resurrection has occurred.
Cellular memory transferred with the transplanted heart produces involuntary reflexes and violent impulses in Maya.
The intruding persona intensifies under stress and in proximity to people and places tied to Marcus's former life.

knows: That she is not possessed by a ghost — she is fighting cell-deep memory that isn't hers for control of her own hands.
hides: How close she has come, in private, to losing that fight entirely — from Daniel, from Sora, from herself most nights.
believes_falsely: Believed for months that no one would ever believe the truth even if she told it; tonight, for the first time, she has to prove it instead of hide it.

knows: That he is memory, not a man risen from the dead — and that the distinction has never mattered to what he wants.
hides: The full extent of how close he has come to erasing her outright — he does not warn her when he is winning.

knows: That resurrection is not real, whatever her people whisper.
hides: How badly her own composure has cracked in private at the idea that some piece of Marcus is truly in that room with her.
believes_falsely: Had convinced herself the myth could be managed and turned to her advantage; tonight it walks through her own red door and she cannot manage it at all.

knows: That the reflexes and stance she's watched Maya use are unmistakably Marcus's own — she trained beside him long enough to know.
hides: How much it costs her to stand in a room with something wearing his instincts and not call it him.
believes_falsely: Had let herself hope, weeks ago, that some real piece of him had come back to her; tonight she finally accepts that he is simply gone, and Maya is not a substitute for grieving him.

case.label: The Marriage
case.solution: Maya's months of lies were meant to protect Daniel from a truth too dangerous and strange to survive telling — but even now that the truth is out, understanding it hasn't undone the damage of what she hid or what she's shown herself capable of. There is no clean way back to who they were before the transplant.
objective_facts: Maya concealed blackouts, a hidden weapon, and violent incidents from Daniel for months.
Daniel has since learned the true nature of the transplant and what it did to her.
Neither Maya nor Daniel has found a way to fully rebuild trust since the truth came out.

knows: Everything she has cost this marriage by choosing silence over the truth, month after month.

believes_falsely: Wants to believe that surviving tonight will be enough to start rebuilding; the episode leaves that hope unresolved rather than answered.

knows: That Maya's blackouts and the violence he's found traces of are connected to the illegal transplant and to whatever survived inside the donor's heart.
hides: His fear that he no longer knows how to love who she's become, holding onto the marriage anyway because he doesn't know how to stop.
believes_falsely: Had believed that naming the truth out loud in recent weeks would be the hardest part, and that things could only get easier from there; tonight proves that understanding a wound doesn't close it.
